page: Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the Intel Xeon 8223CL processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 72 virtual CPUs mapped to 36 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c5.18xlarge displays strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by its top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 3707.05 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 92.77 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance, including Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core metrics, falls within the average range. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip compression and PassMark encryption, also perform at average levels. However, text generation speeds for large language models are weak, reflecting the lack of specialized GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5.18xlarge offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory arrays or local NVMe storage. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or real-time LLM text generation due to the 2.0 GB per core memory constraint and the absence of hardware accelerators.
description: The Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 72 vCPUs (36 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8223CL processor running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, while single-core execution and cryptographic operations perform at average levels. Due to its high compute-to-memory ratio, the c5.18xlarge is suited for multi-threaded processing, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, though it is constrained for memory-heavy or GPU-dependent workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 72 vCPUs, 144.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.18xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 72 vCPUs, 144.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring seventy-two dedicated vCPUs and fast DDR4 memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon CPU
144.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
